questions.text: Why is Laylat al-Qadr described as better than a thousand months?
questions.answer: Because worship in that night carries immense reward beyond a lifetime’s worth of ordinary months.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that one night of worship can outweigh years of worship by Allah’s generosity.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Quran being ‘sent down’ on this night?
questions.answer: It highlights the honor of revelation and that guidance is a gift from Allah to humanity.

questions.text: How does the descent of angels on Laylat al-Qadr affect a believer’s mindset?
questions.answer: It creates awe and hope, motivating worship and repentance because Allah’s mercy is near.

questions.text: What does it mean that angels descend with every matter by Allah’s permission?
questions.answer: Allah’s decree is executed with perfect order; angels carry out what Allah commands.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches angels act independently without Allah’s permission.
questions.answer: False. It emphasizes they descend by their Lord’s permission.

questions.text: What does the Surah’s description of the night as ‘peace’ teach?
questions.answer: It is a night of mercy, safety, and spiritual calm for sincere worshippers.

questions.text: How can a believer seek Laylat al-Qadr without needing to know its exact date?
questions.answer: Strive in worship throughout the last ten nights, especially the odd nights.

questions.text: What is one practical worship plan for the last ten nights inspired by this Surah?
questions.answer: Extra night prayer, Quran, dua, charity, and forgiveness every night, with increased effort on odd nights.

questions.text: Why is hiding the exact date a mercy for believers?
questions.answer: It encourages consistent striving rather than minimal effort on only one expected night.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the value of worship compared to long-term life plans?
questions.answer: One sincere night can outweigh decades, reminding that Allah’s reward is not limited by time.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests Ramadan is mainly cultural and not spiritually unique.
questions.answer: False. It highlights a night of extraordinary spiritual value within Ramadan.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Qadr connect revelation with transformation?
questions.answer: The Quran’s descent is the source of guidance; receiving it should change hearts and behavior.

questions.text: What is one moral lesson from angels descending in great numbers?
questions.answer: The unseen world is real and organized; a believer’s worship is witnessed and honored.

questions.text: How can someone avoid burnout while striving in the last ten nights?
questions.answer: Set a consistent minimum (prayer, Quran, dua) and increase gradually with sincerity, not perfectionism.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that only scholars can benefit from Laylat al-Qadr.
questions.answer: False. Any sincere believer can benefit through worship and repentance.

questions.text: What is one action that best matches the Surah’s theme of forgiveness?
questions.answer: Making abundant dua for forgiveness and forgiving others for Allah’s sake.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that peace lasts ‘until dawn’?
questions.answer: The night’s blessing extends through the night, encouraging consistent worship until morning.

questions.text: What is one way to connect Quran recitation to Laylat al-Qadr meaningfully?
questions.answer: Recite with reflection and make dua to live by the guidance, not only to finish pages.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that decrees make dua useless.
questions.answer: False. Dua and worship are among the greatest means of seeking Allah’s mercy and good outcomes.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Qadr' mean?
questions.answer: Power, Decree, or Honor.

questions.text: What was 'sent down' during the Night of Al-Qadr?
questions.answer: The Quran.

questions.text: In which month does the Night of Al-Qadr occur?
questions.answer: The month of Ramadan.

questions.text: What is the value of the Night of Al-Qadr in terms of time?
questions.answer: It is better than a thousand months.

questions.text: Who descends during this night?
questions.answer: The angels and the Spirit (Angel Jibreel).

questions.text: By whose permission do the angels descend?
questions.answer: By permission of their Lord.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the angels' descent during this night?
questions.answer: To carry out every matter/decree (Kulli Amrin).

questions.text: How is the atmosphere of this night described?
questions.answer: As 'Peace' (Salam).

questions.text: How long does the peace of Laylat al-Qadr last?
questions.answer: Until the emergence of dawn (Fajr).

questions.text: What is the 'Spirit' (Ar-Ruh) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Angel Jibreel (Gabriel).

questions.text: Why is the exact date of Laylat al-Qadr hidden from us?
questions.answer: To encourage believers to strive in worship throughout the last ten nights of Ramadan.

questions.text: What does 'better than a thousand months' imply?
questions.answer: That worship during this single night is more rewarding than worship for over 83 years (a lifetime).

questions.text: What is the root meaning of 'Qadr' regarding the 'narrowing' of the earth?
questions.answer: Some scholars say it is named because the earth becomes 'narrow' (crowded) due to the immense number of angels descending.

questions.text: True or False: Every matter of the upcoming year is decreed during this night.
questions.answer: True, based on this Surah and Surah Ad-Dukhan.

questions.text: Is Laylat al-Qadr a historical event or an annually recurring one?
questions.answer: It is an annually recurring night.

questions.text: What is the special Dua recommended for this night?
questions.answer: 'O Allah, You are forgiving, You love forgiveness, so forgive me' (Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun...).

questions.text: What does the phrase 'And what can make you know' (Wa ma adraka) signify in the Quran?
questions.answer: It signifies the greatness and transcendence of the subject being discussed.

questions.text: How does the Quran describe the beginning of the night's revelation in Surah Ad-Dukhan?
questions.answer: As a 'blessed night'.

questions.text: What is the primary spiritual focus of a believer during this night?
questions.answer: Ibadah (worship), Istighfar (forgiveness), and Tilawah (Quranic recitation).

questions.text: Why is the Surah titled 'Al-Qadr'?
questions.answer: Because it explains the honor of the night the Quran began to descend and the decree of Allah for that night.
